mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient orchestration of large language models. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for adequate hardware resources.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface to manage deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> Install the CLI for managing llm-d deployments.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-cli-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'us-central1' with your preferred region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have the correct permissions to access the cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install llm-d Helm Chart
steps.description:
This command installs the llm-d Helm chart in the 'llm-d' namespace.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

kubectl create namespace llm-d

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --namespace llm-d --version 0.5.1

```

> Add the llm-d Helm repository.
> Create a namespace for llm-d.
> Install the llm-d chart with the specified version.
commands: helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstall the llm-d release.
> Delete the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This process includes draining nodes if necessary, upgrading the Helm chart, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --namespace llm-d --version 0.5.1

kubectl get pods --namespace llm-d

```

> Replace <node-name> with the actual node name.
> Upgrade to the latest version of llm-d.
> Verify that all pods are running successfully.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error creating deployment: context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster has enough resources allocated. Consider resizing your nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified container image could not be found.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in your Helm chart values.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are stuck in 'Pending' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually happens when there are not enough resources available in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Check the resource requests and limits in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that a pod is failing to start repeatedly.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new hardware accelerators and improved scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods --namespace llm-d' showing all pods in 'Running' state.

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
